query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "weak-to-strong generalization via direct on-policy distillation" computer science
rationale: The paper focuses on a specific architectural limitation of Diffusion Language Models (variable-length generation) and proposes a length-regularization framework, which is a distinct mechanism from the "Weak-to-Strong Generalization via Direct On-Policy Distillation" methodology the user is investigating. It does not measure the same causal mechanism, share the same independent/dependent variables, or serve as a foundational reference for the specific distillation-based generalization technique
